titulo: Schools forced to cut back on support for Send pupils in England, poll finds
resumen:
More than 70% have cut down in past year on teaching assistants, who play key role in helping children with Send

Two-fifths of school leaders in England have been forced to cut back on support for children with special educational needs due to a financial crisis “more than a decade in the making”, according to a poll.

Seven out of 10 (71%) leaders say they have cut down on teaching assistants (TAs) in the past year, while 49% have reduced support staff. The crisis could escalate as 81% warn of further cuts in the year ahead.

Se trata de Paolo Zampolli, un antiguo agente de modelos involucrado en el caso Epstein y acusado por su exesposa de haber usado sus influencias para deportarla. Según el operador, la movida serviría para reparar las relaciones entre Trump y Giorgia Meloni.

Los manifestantes piden medidas contra el hambre que afecta a los pueblos indígenas y la reducción de los precios del combustible, en medio del impacto de la guerra en Irán. La medida, enmarcada en la disputa por el rediseño de distritos, busca asegurar 10 de los 11 escaños del estado. Es vista como respuesta a iniciativas republicanas en Texas, Carolina del Norte y Ohio, promovidas por Donald Trump.

titulo: Elecciones históricas en Gaza tras 20 años: paso hacia la unidad palestina
resumen: Tras 20 años, Gaza vuelve a celebrar elecciones. Este fin de semana, 70.000 habitantes de Deir al-Balah, una de las zonas menos afectadas por ofensivas israelíes, están llamados a votar en comicios municipales. Para muchos será su primera vez. Las elecciones también se realizan en Cisjordania, como símbolo de unidad palestina. Aunque Hamás no participa oficialmente, algunos candidatos vinculados sí lo harán.

titulo: ¿Qué busca Trump con los cambios de plazos en Irán?
resumen: El presidente de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, anunció que prolongará indefinidamente el cese el fuego vigente con el Gobierno iraní, advirtiendo que el bloqueo de ingreso y salida de buques a los puertos iraníes persistirá. Posteriormente argumentó que la República Islámica está perdiendo alrededor de 500 millones de dólares diarios con el estrecho de Ormuz cerrado, lo que según Trump, estaría profundizando la crisis económica al interior del país de mayoría persa. Por su parte, los negociadores iraníes aseguraron que dialogarán cuando estén dispuestas las condiciones necesarias.

titulo: Informe desde Caracas: paro universitario en Venezuela por salarios de menos de un dólar mensual
resumen: La Universidad Central de Venezuela cumplió un paro de 24 horas para exigir salarios dignos, al que se sumaron 23 universidades autónomas en todo el país y otras 50 instituciones politécnicas. El salario base de los trabajadores equivale a menos de un dólar mensual y, aunque con bonificaciones puede alcanzar hasta 190 dólares al mes, la canasta básica en Venezuela supera los 600 dólares. Nuestra corresponsal, Daniella Zambrano, amplió la información.

titulo: One person diagnosed with cancer every 80 seconds in UK, report reveals
resumen:
NHS struggling to cope with record numbers, which Cancer Research UK says puts progress on survival rates at risk

The number of people in the UK being diagnosed with cancer has reached a record high, with one person diagnosed every 80 seconds, a report reveals.

Cancer Research UK found that more than 403,000 people were being diagnosed with the disease each year, largely due to a growing and ageing population, as people are more likely to develop cancer as they get older.

titulo: UK to pay France another £660m to curb Channel crossings
resumen:
Three-year deal includes funding for a riot squad to ‘disperse’ people trying to board small boats

The UK government has agreed to pay France another £660m to curb the number of asylum seekers travelling across the Channel, including plans to fund a riot squad to “contain and disperse” people trying to board small boats.

Under a three-year deal to be signed on Thursday by the home secretary, Shabana Mahmood, 1,100 enforcement, intelligence and military officers – an increase of 40% – will be employed to track down smuggling gangs and people seeking refuge.

titulo: El Salvador holds mass trial for 486 alleged members of notorious MS-13 gang
resumen:

A Salvadoran court on Tuesday began a collective trial of 486 alleged gang members, in one of the biggest mass trials under president Nayib Bukele’s crackdown on gang violence through controversial emergency powers.

Prosecutors say the charges against alleged members of the Mara Salvatrucha gang, or MS-13, span more than 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022, including a weekend that was El Salvador’s bloodiest since its civil war.

titulo: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024

Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather.

titulo: Temporary accommodation linked to deaths of 104 children in England in six years
resumen:

titulo: Más de 8.000 migrantes murieron en rutas cada vez más peligrosas en 2025
resumen: El Mediterráneo central sigue siendo la ruta migratoria más letal. Sin embargo, también se han registrado numerosas muertes y desapariciones en la frontera entre Afganistán e Irán, así como en la ruta utilizada por refugiados rohingyas que huyen de Myanmar. En 2025, alrededor de 8.000 personas habrían perdido la vida intentando migrar, según el más reciente informe de la Organización Internacional para las Migraciones (OIM). titulo: ¿Qué se sabe de las amenazas de tiroteos en Argentina contra escuelas?
resumen: En Argentina crece la preocupación por más de una decena de amenazas de tiroteos en varias escuelas del país. Son episodios que suceden un mes después de que un menor disparara y matara a otro en la provincia de Santa Fe, y por los que las autoridades ya han activado los protocolos de seguridad. Para entender porqué está pasando esto hablamos con Alejandro Castro Santander, psicopedagogo, investigador y escritor argentino.

titulo: Informe desde Buenos Aires: Milei presentará reforma electoral ante el Congreso

titulo: Experts call for restrictions on pet flea treatments that harm UK songbirds
resumen:
Chemicals known to affect brains of common garden birds, and to kill unborn chicks, found in most feather samples

Conservationists have called for restrictions on pet flea treatments after research found songbird feathers widely contaminated with substances that can damage the birds’ brains and kill unborn chicks.

Almost every feather sample tested from five common species of UK garden birds contained either permethrin, imidacloprid or fipronil – all insecticides that are banned for agricultural use but still common in pet tick and flea treatments.

titulo: Cattle breed whose ancestors lived with Celts added to priority at-risk list
resumen:
UK’s Rare Breeds Survival Trust says calf numbers of white park cattle last year were less than two-thirds of 2022 level

An ancient breed of cattle whose ancestors are thought to have accompanied the Celts as they were pushed to Britain’s fringes by the Romans has been designated as urgently at risk by a UK conservation charity.

Publishing its 2026 watchlist on Tuesday, the Rare Breeds Survival Trust moved white park cattle to its “priority” category as new calf numbers sank last year to less than two-thirds of their 2022 level.

A Pennsylvania court on Monday said that the state’s constitution guarantees a right to abortion while striking down a decades-long law banning the use of state Medicaid funds to cover abortion costs.

The ruling by a divided seven-judge panel of the appellate-level commonwealth court is a major victory for Planned Parenthood and abortion clinic operators who first sued Pennsylvania over its Medicaid funding restrictions in 2019.

Apple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO after nearly 15 years, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.

“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release.

One Canadian tourist has been killed and at least four other people have been shot after an armed man opened fire at one of Mexico’s most famous tourist destinations, the Teotihuacán pyramids near Mexico City.

The shooting – the latest violent incident to affect Mexico as it prepares to co-host the football World Cup in June – took place on Monday lunchtime and was captured in mobile phone videos shot by visitors to the site.

Boy, 17, and man, 19, detained as Met says it is investigating claims a series of antisemitic attacks are linked to Iran

Two teenagers have been arrested over an arson attack at a synagogue in north-west London over the weekend, the Metropolitan police have said.

Matt Jukes, the deputy commissioner of the Met, said a 17-year-old boy and 19-year-old man had been detained, as he confirmed officers were investigating claims a series of antisemitic attacks in the UK were linked to Iranian-backed proxies.

titulo: Pro-Russian Rumen Radev on course for runaway victory in Bulgarian elections
resumen:
Stronger-than-expected win for former president could end years of weak coalition governments

The pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev is on course for a runaway victory in Bulgaria’s parliamentary elections, official results show, potentially ending years of weak coalition governments and sidelining long-dominant political forces.

The performance, surpassing opinion polls, is one of the strongest results by a single party in a generation and may end, for now, the instability that led to eight elections in five years.

titulo: Trustpilot hosts reviews of illegal casinos, raising concern among MPs

Reviews website features pages praising unlicensed sites’ supposed qualities as if they were legitimate businesses

Trustpilot, the reviews website that offers to “find a company you can trust”, is hosting ratings of illegal casinos, raising concerns in Westminster about the potential risks posed to consumers.

Unlicensed casino operators linked to financial harm, addiction and even suicide have flooded the UK market in recent years, often targeting people who are trying to limit their gambling by “self-excluding” from licensed operators.

titulo: Starwatch: Lyrid meteor shower returns to the spring skies
resumen:
First recorded in 687BC, the meteoroids were once part of the tail of a comet discovered in 1861

This week, the annual Lyrid meteor shower returns to the spring skies. Although active since 16 April, the shower peaks during the late evening of Wednesday 22 April and early the next morning.

The chart shows the view looking east from London at 00.01 (BST) on Thursday 23 April. The origin point of the meteors, known as the radiant, is labelled Lyrids. It sits in the constellation of Lyra, the Lyre, close to the bright star of Vega.

An “exam-obsessed” school system is leaving young people unprepared for work, Alan Milburn has said, as new polling suggests teachers believe pupils are leaving education without the skills they need for adult life.

Milburn, a former cabinet minister under Tony Blair and now leading a government-commissioned review into young people and work, said the system had become overly focused on academic sorting rather than real-world readiness.

It’s Friday, the clock is ticking, and there is a vulnerable child in her care for whom – despite hitting the phones for days – she cannot find a place. titulo: Boca se impone en el Monumental con un gol de Paredes en el superclásico ante River
resumen: Victoria por la mínima del cuadro xeneize en la fecha número 15 de la liga argentina. Terminó así la racha de nueve partidos sin conocer la derrota del combinado 'Millonario', bajo las órdenes del 'Chacho' Coudet. Leandro Paredes celebró en el Monumental con un guiño a Juan Román Riquelme.
